mission.title: opencost: Make namespace annotations/labels available when aggregating by namespace
mission.description: Make namespace annotations/labels available when aggregating by namespace. Requested by 11+ users.

steps.title: Check current opencost deployment
steps.description:
Verify your opencost version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n opencost -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opencost
helm list -n opencost 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working opencost installation.

steps.title: Review opencost configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant opencost configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n opencost -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opencost
kubectl get configmap -n opencost -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=opencost
```
When using the allocations API you should be able to aggregate by namespace and specify explicitly the annotations/labels you want to be included in the properties field.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Make namespace annotations/labels available when…
steps.description:
## What does this PR change?
* This PR implements returning NamespaceLabels and Namespace Annotations in the AllocationProperties object. This allows for separate treatment of the namespace labels/annotations in an Allocation, and avoids having those get clobbered by pod labels/annotations

## How will this PR impact users?
* Existing functionality is unaffected, the labels/annotations fields continue to use the old logic. 

## Does this PR address any GitHub or Zendesk issues?
* Implementation

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n opencost -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opencost
kubectl get events -n opencost --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Make namespace annotations/labels available when…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
## What does this PR change?
* This PR implements returning NamespaceLabels and Namespace Annotations in the AllocationProperties object.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with opencost installed or the issue environment reproducible.
